# Psalms 91

## Verses (16)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Happy is he whose resting-place is in the secret of the Lord, and under the shade of the wings of the Most High; | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Who says of the Lord, He is my safe place and my tower of strength: he is my God, in whom is my hope. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | He will take you out of the bird-net, and keep you safe from wasting disease. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | You will be covered by his feathers; under his wings you will be safe: his good faith will be your salvation. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | You will have no fear of the evil things of the night, or of the arrow in flight by day, | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | Or of the disease which takes men in the dark, or of the destruction which makes waste when the sun is high. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | You will see a thousand falling by your side, and ten thousand at your right hand; but it will not come near you. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | Only with your eyes will you see the reward of the evil-doers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | Because you have said, I am in the hands of the Lord, the Most High is my safe resting-place; | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | No evil will come on you, and no disease will come near your tent. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | For he will give you into the care of his angels to keep you wherever you go. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | In their hands they will keep you up, so that your foot may not be crushed against a stone. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | You will put your foot on the lion and the snake; the young lion and the great snake will be crushed under your feet. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | Because he has given me his love, I will take him out of danger: I will put him in a place of honour, because he has kept my name in his heart. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | When his cry comes up to me, I will give him an answer: I will be with him in trouble; I will make him free from danger and give him honour. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | With long life will he be rewarded; and I will let him see my salvation. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
